task: Implemented direction_confirm handler in stop.py and extension.js to gate autonomous sessions when inference confidence < 0.70, preventing token wastage on ambiguous handovers
discussion_summary: Session resolved a critical insight: handover.json always exists in normal operation, making the 'nothing' signal (confidence 0.35) unreachable. This means direction_confirm (confidence < 0.70) is the only practical gate to prevent autonomous sessions from spinning up without clear direction. User challenged token wastage on direction validation, so the handler now prompts for human input via VSCode input box when confidence is low, rather than auto-starting a session or silently confirming.

accomplishments.what: Built direction_confirm handler in stop.py with human-in-the-loop input box prompt
Updated extension.js to route direction_confirm notifications to VSCode input box UI
Committed both files to main branch

next_actions.action: Test direction_confirm flow end-to-end: trigger a low-confidence scenario (e.g., empty blockers.md, no recent commits), verify VSCode input box appears, and confirm user input is captured and written to handover.json
next_actions.why: Handler is committed but untested in live VSCode environment; need to verify UI integration and input capture work correctly

next_actions.action: Verify that when user provides direction via input box, next autonomous session reads it from handover.json and starts with that direction as Signal 1
next_actions.why: Closes the loop: HITL input must flow into next session's signal chain, otherwise direction_confirm is just a blocker with no recovery path

next_actions.action: Add timeout and fallback behavior to direction_confirm: if user does not respond within 5 minutes, either auto-reject (no session) or escalate to log file for manual review
next_actions.why: Prevents indefinite hangs if user is away; clarifies what happens when HITL gate is triggered but unattended

next_actions.action: Update implementation_state.md to mark Phase 3.13 (direction_confirm HITL gate) as complete, and document the signal chain confidence thresholds in roadmap
next_actions.why: Maintains state tracking and makes the decision to gate at 0.70 confidence explicit for future sessions

decisions.decision: direction_confirm uses VSCode input box (user-provided text) rather than auto-confirming or auto-rejecting
decisions.reason: User challenged token wastage on validation; HITL input box is the only way to recover direction when confidence is low without wasting tokens on guessing

decisions.decision: direction_confirm is the only practical gate; 'nothing' signal (confidence 0.35) is unreachable because handover.json always exists in normal operation
decisions.reason: User's logic: if handover exists, Signal 3 fires at 0.85; if it doesn't, we're in a fresh repo edge case. Either way, direction_confirm at < 0.70 is the real blocker

failed_approaches.approach: Treating 'nothing' signal (confidence 0.35) as the primary gate to prevent token wastage
failed_approaches.reason: User correctly identified that handover.json always exists in normal operation, making this signal unreachable; direction_confirm (< 0.70) is the actual gate

relational_files.why: stop.py calls _infer_direction() from lifecycle.py; direction_confirm handler depends on confidence scores from that module
direction_confirm writes user input to handover.json; next session reads it as Signal 1
blockers.md is a signal source in lifecycle.py; affects confidence calculation that triggers direction_confirm
